hero.title: 5 Days, Mumbai & Goa.
hero.titleAccent: The City of Dreams and India's Favourite Beaches.
hero.intro: The perfect city-and-beach combination. The energy of Mumbai, then the sun, sand and Portuguese charm of Goa. Bollywood and beaches, colonial churches and coastal sunsets, linked by a quick flight.
hero.heroImageAlt: Goa beach and Mumbai skyline combination tour

overview.heading: 5 Days Mumbai + Goa: The Full Picture
body: This 5 day trip pairs India's most energetic city with its most relaxed state. Mumbai brings colonial grandeur, Bollywood and street food; Goa brings golden beaches, Portuguese heritage, seafood and the laid-back coastal vibe that has made it India's favourite holiday spot.

Arranged privately from Malaysia, the package includes the domestic flight between the two, with a private vehicle and driver guide at each end. You experience the highlights of Mumbai, then unwind in Goa, exploring old churches, beaches and the slow pleasures of the coast.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: Mumbai's Energy
items.body: The Gateway of India, colonial landmarks, Marine Drive and a taste of Bollywood, the buzz of India's biggest city in a focused day.

items.title: Goa's Beaches
items.body: Golden sands, palm trees and the Arabian Sea. From the lively beaches of the north to the quieter south, Goa is India's beach paradise.

items.title: Portuguese Heritage
items.body: Old Goa's grand churches, the colourful Latin Quarter of Fontainhas, and the relaxed Portuguese-influenced culture and cuisine.

days.title: Arrive Mumbai
days.description: Met at Mumbai airport and transferred to your hotel. Evening at Marine Drive.

days.title: Mumbai City Tour, Fly to Goa
days.description: Mumbai highlights, then fly to Goa in the evening.

days.title: North Goa & Beaches
days.description: Explore the lively north Goa beaches and forts.

days.title: Old Goa & South Goa
days.description: Portuguese heritage and the quieter southern beaches.

days.title: Depart Goa
days.description: Checkout and transfer to Goa airport for your onward flight.

hotelOptions.heading: Choose Your Comfort Level
hotelOptions.subheading: Centrally located hotels with easy access to the main sights. Pick the tier that suits your style.

whyBook.heading: City and Sea, One Easy Trip
whyBook.body: Combining Mumbai's intensity with Goa's calm means coordinating a flight and two very different stays. We handle the logistics so you simply enjoy the contrast. Dhesu has arranged West India tours for over 35 years.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. We guide you through India e-Visa documentation with every booking.

points.title: Private Tours, Daily Departures
points.body: No minimum group to wait for. Your trip runs on your dates, your pace, your own vehicle and guide.

points.title: A Real Team to Call
points.body: Reachable throughout your trip. Flight delayed, your guide waits. Plans change, we adapt on the ground.

points.title: Group & Custom Pricing
points.body: Travelling as a family or group lowers the per-person cost. Ask us for a tailored quote.

sections.heading: Mumbai & Goa Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
This 5 day package is the ideal city-and-beach combination in West India. Mumbai brings the energy, Bollywood and heritage; Goa brings the beaches, Portuguese charm and relaxation.

The domestic flight links them efficiently, so you experience the best of both without a long overland journey. A private vehicle and driver guide at each end keep things smooth.

It is a popular trip for travellers who want a taste of urban India followed by time to unwind by the sea.

sections.heading: Goa: Beaches and Portuguese Heritage
sections.body:
Goa offers more than beaches. Old Goa's grand churches, like the Basilica of Bom Jesus, are UNESCO-listed, and the Latin Quarter of Fontainhas in Panjim is full of colourful Portuguese-era houses.

The beaches range from the lively north, with its markets and shacks, to the tranquil south. Add in excellent seafood and a relaxed pace, and Goa is the perfect coastal finish to a Mumbai trip.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Mumbai and Goa Airports (BOM / GOI). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: Is the domestic flight included?
items.answer: Yes. This package includes the domestic flight from Mumbai to Goa, so you avoid a long overland journey. International flights to and from India are not included.

items.question: What is the best time to visit Goa?
items.answer: November to February is peak season, with sunny, dry weather perfect for the beaches. March to May is hot. The monsoon (June to September) brings heavy rain and a lush, quiet Goa, with many beach shacks closed.

items.question: Is Goa good for relaxation or activities?
items.answer: Both. Goa suits travellers who want to relax on the beach as well as those who enjoy water sports, nightlife, markets and heritage sightseeing. The trip can be tailored towards relaxation or activity depending on your preference.
